commands.review_summary:
The PR adds sanitized Gateway hook rejection warning logs, stable rejection reason codes, and focused hook mapping/request-handler tests.

PR surface: Source +391, Tests +212. Total +603 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The linked report gives a curl/journal reproduction path, and current main source shows representative hook 4xx branches returning without rejection logs; I did not run a live repro because this review had to keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because no discrete blocker was found; this needs normal maintainer review and author readiness rather than automated code repair.

target.title: fix(gateway): log hook rejection diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

- Add centralized, sanitized warning logs for expected Gateway hook 4xx rejections.
- Preserve existing hook HTTP statuses and response bodies while adding stable rejection reason codes.
- Add focused regression coverage for body-read, direct validation, and mapped validation rejection logs.

Fixes #69192.

## AI assistance disclosure

This PR was prepared with Codex assistance. I reviewed and understand the affected Gateway hook request path, including `src/gateway/server/hooks-request-handler.ts`, `src/gateway/server/hooks-rejection-log.ts`, `src/gateway/hooks.ts`, and the focused tests.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Gateway hook requests that return 4xx now emit one sanitized warning line with stable diagnostic fields, so operators can diagnose body-read, direct validation, and mapped validation failures from the journal/log.

- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Gateway runtime on loopback with throwaway `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`; fake hook token; no real credentials.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```bash
  OPENCLAW_HOME=.artifacts/issue-69192-proof-rebase.0JNYUV/home \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=.artifacts/issue-69192-proof-rebase.0JNYUV/state \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=.artifacts/issue-69192-proof-rebase.0JNYUV/openclaw.json \
  OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug \
  pnpm openclaw gateway run --port 33583 --bind loopback --allow-unconfigured --auth none --verbose

  curl -sS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:33583/hooks/wake \
    -H 'content-type: application/json' \
    -H 'x-request-id: proof-invalid-json' \
    --data-binary 'not-json'

  curl -sS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:33583/hooks/agent \
    -H 'content-type: application/json' \
    -H 'x-request-id: proof-direct-invalid-channel' \
    --data-binary '{"message":"hello from proof","channel":"last","agent":"proof"}'

  curl -sS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:33583/hooks/mapped-message \
    -H 'content-type: application/json' \
    -H 'x-request-id: proof-mapped-scalar' \
    --data-binary '"hello from scalar proof"'
  ```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  Copied live output and redacted runtime log excerpt from the isolated Gateway run:

  ```text
  invalid_json status=400 body={"ok":false,"error":"Unexpected token 'o', \"not-json\" is not valid JSON"}
  direct_validation status=400 body={"ok":false,"error":"channel must be last"}
  mapped_scalar status=400 body={"ok":false,"error":"hook mapping requires message"}

  2026-05-25T14:56:38.517-04:00 [hooks] hook rejected path=wake code=400 reason=body-invalid-json method=POST content-type=application/json body-type=unparsed body-bytes=8 request-id=proof-invalid-json
  2026-05-25T14:56:38.529-04:00 [hooks] hook rejected path=agent code=400 reason=agent-channel-invalid method=POST content-type=application/json body-type=object body-bytes=58 request-id=proof-direct-invalid-channel
  2026-05-25T14:56:38.542-04:00 [hooks] hook rejected path=mapped-message code=400 reason=mapping-requires-message method=POST content-type=application/json body-type=string body-bytes=23 request-id=proof-mapped-scalar

  log_leak_check=PASS
  status_checks=PASS
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The three representative hook 4xx responses remained 400, and each emitted a journal-visible `[hooks] hook rejected ...` warning with path, status code, stable reason, method, content type, body type, bounded body length, and request id. The warning lines did not contain the fake token or sensitive payload strings.

- What was not tested: Live third-party webhook senders, successful hook dispatch into an agent run, and external channel delivery. The changed surface is rejection diagnostics; the proof intentionally avoided real credentials and live integrations.

- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Proof used loopback Gateway runtime and throwaway state/config only. It did not use real hook credentials or live external integrations.

## Root Cause

The hook request handler returned expected 4xx responses from several branches without a corresponding rejection log. Body-read failures, direct `wake`/`agent` validation failures, mapped hook validation failures, policy/session-key failures, and final hook 404s therefore had user-visible failures but weak operator diagnostics.

## Dependency contract

`src/gateway/hooks.ts` wraps `src/infra/http-body.ts`. The body reader returns stable limit codes and current response strings:

- `PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE` -> `payload too large`
- `REQUEST_BODY_TIMEOUT` -> `request body timeout`
- `CONNECTION_CLOSED` -> `Connection closed`
- invalid JSON -> parser error text

The patch maps these existing strings to stable hook rejection reasons without changing the response body or status mapping.

`src/logging/subsystem.ts` accepts `logHooks.warn(message, meta)`. Because console/journal logs surface the message most directly, the patch puts the sanitized key/value diagnostics in the warning message and also passes structured metadata.

## Regression Test Plan

- `src/gateway/server-http.hooks-request-timeout.test.ts` now covers warning logs for request body timeout, invalid JSON, direct validation rejection, and mapped validation rejection.
- The tests assert stable reason codes, request ids, body types, bounded body length, and absence of fake tokens/raw payload strings in warning messages and metadata.
- `src/gateway/hooks-mapping.test.ts` now asserts stable mapping reasons for missing agent message and wake text.

## Security Impact

Positive. The new logs improve diagnosability without logging Authorization headers, hook tokens, raw request bodies, rendered hook messages, channel targets, or session keys. Header-derived log values are normalized to one line and length bounded. Body length is reported from `content-length` only and capped.

## Human Verification

I reviewed the affected hook ingress path, the body reader contract, and the logging helper contract. I also reviewed and accepted the `codex review --base origin/main` finding about scalar body-type inference, then reran tests, changed checks, and review. After rebasing onto current upstream main, I reran the focused tests, changed gate, and isolated Gateway proof.

## CODEOWNERS / owner review

`.github/CODEOWNERS` has secops ownership for gateway auth/secret/security-specific files, but no direct required owner for these touched hook handler/mapping/test files. Gateway maintainer review is still recommended because this changes hook ingress logging semantics.

## Changelog

No `CHANGELOG.md` edit. This is a contributor PR; maintainer/AI landing should add the release changelog entry if accepted.

## Review conversations

- Checked issue #69192 and exact open/closed PR searches.
- No open PR linked to or substantially addressing #69192 was found.
- Reviewed closed PR #69267 as prior art; it was closed unmerged and had broader/unrelated changes.
- Ran `codex review --base origin/main`; accepted the scalar body-type finding.
- Reran `codex review --base origin/main`; it reported no discrete actionable regressions.

## Risks

- The patch intentionally adds one warning log for each expected hook 4xx rejection, which may increase warning volume for misconfigured or noisy webhook senders.
- Stable reason codes are now an operator-facing diagnostic surface; future hook rejection branches should use the same helper and add focused coverage.

## Behavior tradeoffs

- The warning message favors compact grep-friendly key/value fields over verbose prose so it is useful in plain journal/console output.
- Invalid JSON response bodies still include parser text as before, but the new warning line intentionally does not include that parser text to avoid echoing raw body snippets into logs.

## Scope boundaries

- Does not change hook auth behavior, status codes, response bodies, mapping selection, dispatch behavior, channel delivery, or session-key policy.
- Does not add a new config option or dependency.
- Does not broaden hook payload logging beyond sanitized metadata.

## Validation

```bash
pnpm test src/gateway/server-http.hooks-request-timeout.test.ts src/gateway/hooks-mapping.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm check:changed
codex review --base origin/main
```

Focused tests: 2 files, 36 tests passed.
`pnpm check:changed`: passed core/coreTests lanes.
Final `codex review --base origin/main`: no discrete actionable regressions.
Rebase proof: branch rebased onto `f00a912c25`; focused tests, `check:changed`, and isolated Gateway proof passed after rebase.
